definition: The regularity or lawful invariability of dhamma-patterns.
notes: The repository keeps regularity of dhamma as the default because it keeps the family tie audible and works well beside dhammatā and dhammatthiti. Regularity of the pattern remains acceptable when smoother English is needed. The Buddhadasa usage profile and the Idappaccayatā practical talk profile support this term as part of the natural-law support layer around conditionality and translator-facing anti-drift notes. The term should not be softened into mere tendency or inflated into fate.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where smoother English helps
context_rules.notes: Use when the lawful recurrence is the point and keeping dhamma audible matters less.

authority_basis.scope: Supports lawful-regularity handling in the dhamma family.
Supports treating dhammaniyāmatā as part of the natural-law support layer around idappaccayatā.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'regularity of dhamma' as the default rendering unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the term into vague tendency-language or deterministic fate-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift into fate-language or weak paraphrase that hides the lawful regularity.
